| I46_Brainage | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Brain MRI | Brain age |  | brn_age_i46p1 | 60238 |  | Age at brain scan (years) - Cross-sectional - Insight 46 Phase 1 | {-9.0} No brain scan | years | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | No | Yes | Contains age information. | Insight46 phase 1 brain-predicted age was produced for all participants with a T1 MRI scan which passed quality control.  |
| I46_Brainage | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Brain MRI | Brain age |  | brn_bpa_i46p1 | 60239 |  | Adjusted baseline brain-predicted age - Cross-sectional - Insight 46 Phase 1 | {-9.0} No brain scan | years | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | Yes | Yes | Contains age information. | Insight46 phase 1 brain-predicted age was produced for all participants with a T1 MRI scan which passed quality control. The bl_bpa_adjusted variable is the brain-predicted age after adjustment for age bias. The age bias adjustment was performed by subtracting 3.33 from the unadjusted brain age and dividing by 0.91. These values were the intercept and slope of the model in the training dataset of brain-predicted age regressed on chronological age. |
| I46_Brainage | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Brain MRI | Brain age |  | brn_bpaun_i46p1 | 60240 |  | Unadjusted baseline brain-predicted age - Cross-sectional - Insight 46 Phase 1 | {-9.0} No brain scan | years | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | Yes | Yes | Contains age information. | Insight46 phase 1 brain-predicted age was produced for all participants with a T1 MRI scan which passed quality control.  |

| I46_Brainage | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Brain MRI | Brain age |  | brn_pad_i46p1 | 60242 |  | Adjusted baseline brain predicted age differeince (Brain-PAD) - Cross-sectional - Insight 46 Phase 1 | {-9.0} No brain scan | years | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | Yes | Yes | Contains age information. | Insight46 phase 1 brain-predicted age was produced for all participants with a T1 MRI scan which passed quality control. Brain-predicted age difference (Brain-PAD) is provided for the adjusted brain-predicted age estimates only. This was calculated by subtracting chronological age from adjusted brain-predicted age. |
| I46P2_BP | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Physical measures | Blood pressure |  | sit_syst1_i46p2 | 61429 |  | First sitting systolic blood pressure after 3 minutes of rest (mmHg) - Insight46 phase 1 | {-9.0} Unknown | mmHg | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | No | No |  | Insight46 phase 2 blood pressure and pulse rate measures. Measurements were performed twice (one minute apart) in the left arm after 3 minutes of sitting down, and then twice (one minute apart) following 3 minutes in the recumbent position, and then twice (one minute apart) following 3 minutes of standing quietly. Lying and standing measures were also taken in phase 1 - see library file I46_bloodPressure. |

| I46_braintap | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Physical activity | Bradykinesia Akinesia Incoordination (BRAIN) Tap Test |  | bt_ds_lft15_i46p1 | 60647 |  | Dysmetria score (DS) - Weighted index of number of incorrect key taps, scored in a target fashion, then divided by the total number of key taps  - left hand- Insight46 phase 1 | {-9.0} Unknown   {999.0} Missing | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 1 BRAIN test: a validated test for detecting signs of Parkinson's disease and other neurological conditions. Using a Lenovo Thinkpad laptop, participants were instructed to tap as fast and accurately as possible, alternating between the s and ; keys for thirty seconds, with each hand separately. In several cases, the hand used for each test may have been recorded incorrectly (indicated by a discrepancy between the data recorded by staff and the website).  This discrepancy has been coded with the variable “BT_rightleftuncertain_15“,the data is useable for analyses using a the mean of both hands, or “worst hand” analyses, but should be excluded if relationships with dominant or non-dominant hands are investigated. Also performed at phase 2 - see library file I46P2_braintap. |

Using a Lenovo Thinkpad laptop, participants were instructed to tap as fast and accurately as possible, alternating between the s and ; keys for thirty seconds, with each hand separately. In several cases, the hand used for each test may have been recorded incorrectly (indicated by a discrepancy between the data recorded by staff and the website).  This discrepancy has been coded with the variable “BT_rightleftuncertain_15“,the data is useable for analyses using a the mean of both hands, or “worst hand” analyses, but should be excluded if relationships with dominant or non-dominant hands are investigated. Also performed at phase 2 - see library file I46P2_braintap. |

| I46P2_Lumipulse | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Biological samples | Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) biomarkers |  | ab40_lumi_i46p2 | 61071 |  | Ab40 biomarker measured in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) using Lumipulse assays - Insight46 phase 2 | {-9.0} Unknown   {-99.0} Unknown | pg/ml | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | No | No |  | Insight46 phase 2 CSF biomarker analysis - details of the commercially available Lumipulse AB40, AB42, p-tau181 and t-tau assays are given in the kit inserts and they were all used as per the manufacturer’s instructions. Measurements were undertaken in singlicate, using two reagent batches: the first 72 samples were assayed in 2019 for an interim analysis (1) and the remaining 62 samples were assayed in 2021. CSF was not collected at phase 1. |

| I46_memory | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Health and medical history | Personal history of neurological disorder |  | low_digit_symbol_i46p1 | 61277 |  | Did this participant have a low score (<= -1.5 z-score) on the Logical Memory Delayed recall test? - Insight46 phase 1 | {-9.0} Unknown   {-99.0} Missing value   {0.0} No   {1.0} Yes   {2.0} Not known | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 1 mild cognitive impairment (MCI) / dementia consensus. Objective evidence of  an amnestic deficit (Logical Memory delayed recall ≥ 1.5 SD below the mean). Also collected during phase 2 - see library file I46P2_NeuroPsyc_MMSE. |

| I46_memory | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Health and medical history | Personal history of neurological disorder |  | low_logical_memory_i46p1 | 61278 |  | Did this participant have a low score (<= -1.5 z-score) on the Digit-symbol substitution test? - Insight46 phase 1 | {-9.0} Unknown   {-99.0} Missing value   {0.0} No   {1.0} Yes   {2.0} Not known | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 1 mild cognitive impairment (MCI) / dementia consensus. Objective evidence of a non-amnestic deficit (Digit-Symbol Substitution score ≥ 1.5 SD below the mean). This cognitive test was chosen for defining a cognitive deficit on the basis of its normal distribution across the entire cohort. Also collected in phase 2 - see library file I46P2_NeuroPsyc_MMSE. |
| I46_memory | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Health and medical history | Personal history of neurological disorder |  | mci_i46p1 | 61279 |  | Participant meets criteria for Mild Cognitive Impairment - Insight46 phase 1 | {-9.0} Unknown   {-99.0} Missing value   {0.0} No   {1.0} Yes   {2.0} Not known | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 1 mild cognitive impairment (MCI) / dementia consensus. Criteria for Mild cognitive impairment (MCI) were agreed based on published criteria. The principle of these criteria is that they require an absence of dementia AND evidence of objective cognitive impairment on one or more neuropsychological tests AND subjective cognitive concerns reported by the participant or an informant.  Also collected in phase 2 - see library file I46P2_NeuroPsyc_Cognitive. |
| I46P2_neuropsychology | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Health and medical history | Personal history of neurological disorder |  | mci_i46p2 | 61279 |  | Participant meets criteria for Mild Cognitive Impairment - Insight46 phase 2 | {-9.0} Unknown   {0.0} No   {1.0} Yes | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | No | No |  | Insight46 phase 2 mild cognitive impairment. Criteria for mild cognitive impairment (MCI) were agreed based on published criteria. The principle of these criteria is that they require an absence of dementia AND evidence of objective cognitive impairment on one or more neuropsychological tests AND subjective cognitive concerns reported by the participant or an informant. It was determined using phase 2 data only. Also derived at phase 1 - see library file I46_memory. |

| I46_Vascular_medication | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Health and medical history | Medications  |  | vasc_meddiuretic_i46p1 | 61215 |  | Presence of Diuretics. These drugs may be prescribed to promote renal function and offload fluid (for example in heart failure) or to lower blood pressure. The group includes thiazide like diuretics (BNF221), loop diuretics (BNF222), potassium sparing diuretics (BNF223, BNF224) and other diuretics. Includes BNF groups 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4, 2.2.5, 2.2.6, 2.2.7 & 2.2.8. - Insight46 phase 1 | {-9.0} Unknown   {0.0} No medication   {1.0} Presence of medication | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 1 medications taken within the last 24 hours, self-reported by participant. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.  |
| I46P2_PSQI | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Health and medical history | Sleep |  | psqidaysdys_i46p2 | 61362 |  | Days of dysfunction due to sleepiness. Categorized such that if totalscore_i46p2 is = 0 then psqidaysdys_i46p2 = 0, ≥ 1 and ≤ 2 = 1, ≥ 3 and ≤ 4 = 2, and ≥ 5 and ≤ 6 = 3. Min score = 0 (better) and max score = 3 (worse). In PSQI scoring tool, psqidaysdys_i46p2 variable is known as PSQIDAYDYS.  - Insight46 phase 2 | {-9.0} Unknown   {-99.0} Missing   {0.0} Better   {1.0} One   {2.0} Two   {3.0} Worse | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. Eight domains are assessed including sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, habitual sleep efficiency, sleep disturbance, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.  |
| I46P2_PSQI | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Health and medical history | Sleep |  | psqidistb_i46p2 | 61363 |  | Sleep disturbance. Categorized such that if sumscore_i46p2 = 0 then psqidistb_i46p2 = 0, ≥ 1 and ≤ 9 = 1, > 9 and ≤ 18 = 2, and > 18 = 3. Min score = 0 (better) and max score = 3 (worse). In PSQI scoring tool, psqidistb_i46p2 variable is known as PSQIDISTB.  - Insight46 phase 2 | {-9.0} Unknown   {-99.0} Missing   {0.0} Better   {1.0} One   {2.0} Two   {3.0} Worse | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. | I46P2_PSQI | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Health and medical history | Sleep |  | psqidurat_i46p2 | 61364 |  | Sleep duration. Categorized such that if actslpdur_i46p2 ≥ 7 then psqidurat_i46p2 = 0, < 7 and ≥ 6 = 1, <6 and ≥ 5 = 2, and < 5 = 3. Min score = 0 (better) and max score = 3 (worse). In PSQI scoring tool, psqidurat_i46p2 variable is known as PSQUDURAT.  - Insight46 phase 2 | {-9.0} Unknown   {-99.0} Missing   {0.0} >=7   {1.0} >=6 and <7   {2.0} >=5 and <6   {3.0} <5 | hours | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | No | No |  | Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. A global score is derived as the sum of the domain score. A global PSQI>5 represents poor sleep quality. Not performed at phase 1.  |
| I46P2_PSQI | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Health and medical history | Sleep |  | psqihse_i46p2 | 61365 |  | Sleep efficiency.  Categorized such that if tmphse_i46p2 is ≥ 85 then psqihse_i46p2 = 0, < 85 and ≥ 75 = 1, <75 and ≥ 65 = 2, and < 65 = 3. Min score = 0 (better) and max score = 3 (worse). In PSQI scoring tool, psqihse_i46p2 variable is known as PSQIHSE.  - Insight46 phase 2 | {-9.0} Unknown   {-99.0} Missing   {0.0} >85   {1.0} >=75 and <85   {2.0} >=65 and <75   {3.0} <65 | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. | I46P2_PSQI | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Health and medical history | Sleep |  | slpdur_i46p2 | 61367 |  | Derived by finding difference in seconds between bedtime_i46p2 and day wakeuptime_i46p2 and dividing by 3600 to get difference in hours. If >24, then slpdur_i46p2 = difference in hours minus 24, if ≤24, then slpdur_i46p2 = difference in hours.  In PSQI  scoring tool, slpdur_i46p2 variable is nown as newtib. - Insight46 phase 2 | {-9.0} Unknown   {-99.0} Missing | hours | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | No | No |  | Insight46 phase 2 Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI): designed to assess sleep quality and sleep distubance within the last month. | I46PTQ | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Mental health | Negative thinking |  | summedscore_ptqi46p2 | 61391 |  | SummedScore (total score out of 60) - Perseverative Thinking Questionnaire (PTQ) - Insight46 phase 2 | {-9.0} Unknown | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 2 Perseverative Thinking Questionnaire (PTQ): assesses repetitive negative thinking; consists of 15 statements, and participants are asked to rate the extent to which each statement applies to them on a scale from 0 (never) to 4 (almost always). | I46_memory | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Cognitive function | Informant interviews |  | ad8_appointments_i46p1 | 61395 |  | AD8 informant questionnaire: Trouble remembering appointments - Insight46 phase 1 | {-9.0} Unknown   {-99.0} Missing value   {0.0} No   {1.0} Yes   {2.0} Not known | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | No | No |  | Insight46 phase 1 AD8 - a short interview designed to be administered to a participant's informant to distinguish those with very mild dementia from those without dementia. A response of “Yes, a change” indicates that there has been a change in the last several years caused by cognitive (thinking and memory) problems. An AD8 total score (ad8_totrec_i46p1) greater than or equal to 2 indicates that cognitive impairment is likely to be present. Also collected at phase 2 - see library file I46P2_AD8. |

| I46P2_CDR | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Cognitive function | Informant interviews |  | cdr_score_i46p2 | 61419 |  | Overall Clinical dementia rating (CDR) score - Insight46 phase 2 | {-9.0} Unknown | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 2 clinical dementia rating (CDR) - a global scale which assesses dementia severity via an interview with participant and informant. Performance is rated in six domains (memory, orientation, judgement and problem solving, community activities, home and hobbies, and personal care) and then a global CDR score is calculated by combining the scores from each domain using an establish scoring algorithm. Not measured at phase 1.  |

| I46P2_neuropsychology | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Cognitive function | Reaction time |  | rmrt_i46p2 | 61462 |  | Choice reaction time (RT): Mean RT for correct trials - Insight46 phase 2 | {-9.0} Unknown | milliseconds | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 2 Two related tasks – Choice Reaction Time (RT) and Response Inhibition – were combined into one experiment. The Choice RT task had 2 blocks of 12 trials each. The Response Inhibition task were 2 blocks of 48 trials each. For both tasks, participants were instructed to press the correct button on the response box (left or right) as quickly as possible, according to the stimulus on the screen. Also collected at phase 1 - see I46_neuropsychology. |

| I46P2_neuropsychology | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Cognitive function | Visuomotor performance |  | crateo_i46p2 | 61481 |  | Circle tracing: mean subtraction rate (responses per second) - Insight46 phase 2 | {-9.0} Unknown | response/sec | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 2 circle-tracing and serial subtraction task. Participants were instructed to trace clockwise round the circle using a stylus as quickly and accurately as possible. In the direct condition, participants could see their hand and the path they were tracing on the tablet screen. In the indirect condition, the laptop was covered by an upturned box with the front open to allow the participant to put their hand inside.The task was administered in a single-task format (no subtraction) and a dual-task format with a concurrent task of serial subtraction. Also collected at phase 1 - see library file I46_neuropsychology |

| I46_neuropsychology | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Cognitive function | Preclinical Alzheimer Cognitive Composite (PACC) |  | pacc_i46p1 | 61649 |  | Preclinical Alzheimer Cognitive Composite (PACC) - Insight46 phase 1 | {-9.0} Unknown | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 1 Preclinical Alzheimer Cognitive Composite (PACC) - The four components of our version of the PACC were: MMSE total score, Logical Memory delayed recall score, Digit-Symbol Substitution score and FNAME-total. The four components were converted into z-scores based on the full Insight 46 sample and the four z-scores were then averaged. This has been recalculated at phase 2 - see library file I46P2_NeuroPsyc_MMSE. |

This was also calculated at phase 1 - see library file I46_neuropsychology. |

| I46_bamos | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Brain MRI | White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes | White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes - Cross-sectional | lesiontot_i46p1 | 60197 |  | Global white matter hyperintensity (WMH) volume (ml). Includes subcortical grey matter but not the infratentorial region - Cross-sectional - Insight46 phase 1 | {-9.0} Unknown   {-99.0} Unknown / no data | ml | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 1 white matter hyperintensity volumes (global and regional) generated using the cross-sectional Bayesian Model Selection (BaMoS) pipeline . Note that there is also a longitudinal phase 1 white matter hyperintensity volume dataset which was generated after phase 2 data collection was complete - library file I46P2_Bamos_L. For longitudinal analysis of WMH volume change, we recommend using the I46P2_Bamos_L dataset rather than a combination of cross-sectional measurements of WMH volume from Phase 1 and Phase 2. This is because WMH volumes calculated in a longitudinal framework from mid-point registered images tend to be a more conservative estimate of WMH volume, incorporating information from both time-points.  |

| I46_bamos | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Brain MRI | White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes | White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes - Cross-sectional | bamosfailreason_i46p1 | 60186 |  | Reason why a segmentation was not useable (only relevant if bamosuseable_i46p1=0) - Cross-sectional - Insight46 phase 1 | {-9.0} Unknown   {-99.0} No failure   {1.0} T1 fail   {2.0} FLAIR fail   {3.0} Bamos segmentation fail   {4.0} Cortical stroke mis-segmented   {5.0} Demyelination mis-segmented   {6.0} Other vascular anomaly mis-segmented | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | No | No |  | Insight46 phase 1 white matter hyperintensity volumes (global and regional) generated using the cross-sectional Bayesian Model Selection (BaMoS) pipeline. Note that there is also a longitudinal phase 1 white matter hyperintensity volume dataset which was generated after phase 2 data collection was complete - library file I46P2_Bamos_L. |
| I46P2_Bamos_L | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Brain MRI | White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes | White matter hyperintsity (WMH) volumes - Longitudinal (phase 1 and 2) | bamos_wmhvc_i46p2 | 60214 |  | Change in white matter hyperintensity (WMH) volume from baseline to follow-up (p2_bamos_wmhv_long – p1_bamos_wmhv_long) - Longitudinal (phase 1 and 2 only) - Insight46 phase 2 | {-9.0} Unknown | ml | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 1 & 2 white matter hyperintensity volumes  generated using the longitudinal Bayesian Model Selection (BaMoS) pipeline. These data are intended for longitudinal analysis, and should be used to reflect WMH volume change between Phase 1 and Phase 2. For cross-sectional analysis of Phase 1 global WMH volume, use the variable lesiontot_i46p1. For cross-sectional analysis using only Phase 2 WMH volumes, use the cross-sectional Phase 2 WMHV variable. This is because WMH volumes calculated in a longitudinal framework from mid-point registered images tend to be a more conservative estimate of WMH volume, incorporating information from both time-points. If only interested in WMH volume at a single time-point, cross-sectional measurements are more appropriate. For longitudinal analysis of WMH volume change, it is recommended that only this dataset is used, rather than a combination of cross-sectional measurements of WMH volume from Phase 1 and Phase 2.  For phase 1 cross-sectional measurements, see file handle I46_bamos. |
| I46_amyloidstatus_crb | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Brain PET | Amyloid data | Cross-sectional beta-amyloid status (phase 1) | suvr_crb_i46p1 | 60243 |  | Global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVR) with whole cerebellum reference region - Insight 46 phase 1 | {-9.0} Unknown | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | Yes | No |  | Florbetapir-PET global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVRs) were calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to a whole cerebellum reference region and with and without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff (non-PVC > 1.0770 and PVC > 1.0306). See library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_erodedWM for SUVR and amyloid-beta status calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to an eroded white matter reference region. This pipeline has been performed for the cross-sectional analysis of SUVR only.  For longitudinal pipelines (Phase 1 baseline and Phase 2 follow-up), see file handle I46P2_AmyloidPET for longitudinal values. |

See library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_erodedWM for SUVR and amyloid-beta status calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to an eroded white matter reference region. This pipeline has been performed for the cross-sectional analysis of SUVR only.  For longitudinal pipelines (Phase 1 baseline and Phase 2 follow-up), see file handle I46P2_AmyloidPET for longitudinal values. |

| I46_amyloidstatus_erodedwm | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Brain PET | Amyloid data | Cross-sectional beta-amyloid status (phase 1) | suvr_i46p1 | 60249 |  | Global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVR) with eroded white matter (WM) reference region - Insight 46 Phase 1 | {-9.0} Unknown | Not applicable | Insight46 - Phase 1 | Not applicable | 2015-2018 | Yes | No |  | Florbetapir-PET global standardised uptake value ratios (SUVRs) were calculated from a composite cortical target region normalised to an eroded white matter reference region and with and without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique.Positive  or  negative  amyloid status  was  determined  using  a  Gaussian  mixture  model  applied  to  SUVRs,  with  the  99th  percentile  of  the  lower  Gaussian  as  the  cutoff  (0·6104). | I46P2_AmyloidPET | Sub-Studies | Insight46 | Brain PET | Amyloid data | Longitudinal beta-amyloid status (phase 1 and 2) | ce_cmp_wc_bl_i46p2 | 60255 |  | Centiloid value from SUVRs using composite target region with whole cerebellum reference region - Longitudinal (phase 1 and 2 only) - Insight46 phase 1 | {-888.0} Not applicable   {-9.0} Unknown | CL | Insight46 - Phase 2 | Not applicable | 2018-2021 | Yes | No |  | Insight46 phase 1 & 2 amyloid-beta PET [18F]florbetapir data quantified using longitudinal pipeline. Global and regional SUVRs were calculated normalised to a whole cerebellum, pons or eroded subcortical white matter reference region as indicated and with or without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff. Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset  - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum. |

Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset  - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum. |

Global and regional SUVRs were calculated normalised to a whole cerebellum, pons or eroded subcortical white matter reference region as indicated and with or without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff. Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset  - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum. |

Global and regional SUVRs were calculated normalised to a whole cerebellum, pons or eroded subcortical white matter reference region as indicated and with or without partial volume correction (PVC) using the Discrete Iterative Yang technique. Positive or negative amyloid status was determined using a Gaussian mixture model applied to SUVRs, with the 99th percentile of the lower Gaussian as the cutoff. Note that there is also a cross-sectional phase 1 amyloid dataset  - see library file I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_ErodedWM and I46_AMYLOIDSTATUS_Cerebellum. |
